title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bobby, Frankie, and Baxter (referred to as Baz or Budge) conclude that the man they believed to be "Dr. Nicholson" is actually Roger Baskington-French in disguise. Baxter notes that Baskington-French is a dishonest man and a skilled actor who has previously forged his father's signature. Frankie observes that a letter sent in Bobby's name was highly convincing, suggesting Baskington-French may have gained access to Bobby's handwriting through his connection to the Kemm family.

To confront the imposter, Bobby devises an ambush: Frankie will sit in a chair to appear unsuspecting, while Bobby and Baxter hide behind the door. Baxter explains how he arrived at the house, recounting how he followed Bobby's green Bentley to Stafili to escape creditors. After seeing the car parked at a pub, Baxter became trapped in a garage at Tudor Cottage, eventually escaping by climbing onto the roof to reach the attic.

Bobby expresses his gratitude to Baxter for his timely arrival. As footsteps approach and candlelight appears under the door, the group prepares for the confrontation. When Baskington-French enters the room, Frankie remains slumped in her chair while Bobby and Baxter pounce on him. The ambush is successful; the men knock Baskington-French to the ground and bind him with rope. Bobby triumphantly addresses the captured man, remarking that it is a lovely night for a funeral.
